text: The terms fundamentalist and fundamentalism were coined within the Northern Baptist Convention in 1920 to describe the more conservative theology of modernism. This entry discusses how the terms were used as labels for advocates of orthodoxy of the "fundamentals" of the faith, and how the terms were “assailed from all sides” by liberal Christians, falling prey to the processes of pejoration and lexical extension. By 1923 the term fundamentalist was appearing in political journalism to describe adherents of any number of rigid secular belief systems. It would not be until the 1925 Scopes monkey trial that the terms would be solidified as an ideological reference point in the secular mindset of the public in North America and the United Kingdom, with connotations of anti-intellectualism and obstinacy.
headline: Fully Human Written
